footer.copyright: TaskOps · Built by Alessandro Rodriguez Rojas

dataCollected.title: Information We Collect
items: Email address, used for account authentication.
Task data you create (titles, descriptions, due dates, priorities, and statuses).
UI preferences stored locally in your browser (theme, language, sidebar state).

howWeUse.title: How We Use Your Information
items: To provide and maintain the task management service.
To authenticate your identity and protect your account.
To remember your preferences across sessions.

thirdParties.title: Third-Party Services
items: TaskOps uses Supabase as its authentication and database provider. Your account data and tasks are stored in Supabase-hosted infrastructure.
We do not use analytics, advertising, or tracking services of any kind.

storage.title: Data Storage
items: Account and task data are stored securely in a Supabase-hosted database.
Authentication session tokens are managed by the Supabase SDK and stored in your browser's local storage.
UI preferences (theme, language, view mode) are stored in your browser's local storage and never sent to any server.

rights.title: Your Rights
items: You can delete your account at any time from the account settings page.
You can request information about the data we store by contacting us.
You can clear all locally stored preferences by clearing your browser's local storage.

contact.title: Contact
contact.content: If you have questions about this privacy policy, you can reach us through the contact information provided in the Legal Notice page.

description.title: Service Description
items: TaskOps is a free task management application that allows you to create, organize, and track tasks.
The service is provided for personal productivity purposes.

responsibilities.title: User Responsibilities
items: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials.
You agree not to misuse the service or attempt to access it through unauthorized means.
You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your account.

ip.title: Intellectual Property
items: You retain full ownership of the content and data you create within TaskOps.
The TaskOps platform, its design, and source code remain the property of its creator.

availability.title: Service Availability
items: TaskOps is provided on an "as-is" basis without guarantees of availability or uptime.
We reserve the right to modify, suspend, or discontinue the service at any time.

termination.title: Account Termination
items: You may delete your account at any time through the account settings.
We may terminate accounts that violate these terms or engage in abusive behavior.

liability.title: Limitation of Liability
items: TaskOps and its creator shall not be held liable for any damages arising from the use or inability to use the service.
This includes, but is not limited to, loss of data, interruption of service, or any indirect damages.

changes.title: Changes to These Terms
changes.content: We may update these terms from time to time. Changes will be reflected on this page with an updated date.

overview.title: Overview
items: TaskOps does not use HTTP cookies for tracking, analytics, or advertising purposes.
This page explains what technologies we do use and how they differ from cookies.

localStorage.title: Local Storage (Not Cookies)
items: Local storage is a browser technology that allows websites to store data locally on your device. Unlike cookies, this data is never automatically sent to any server with your requests.
TaskOps uses local storage to remember your UI preferences: theme (light or dark), language (English or Spanish), sidebar state, and task view mode.
These preferences are essential for the application to function as expected and are stored only on your device.

supabaseAuth.title: Authentication Tokens
items: When you sign in, the Supabase authentication SDK stores session tokens in your browser's local storage.
These tokens are used solely to keep you signed in and are managed automatically by the authentication library.
They are not cookies and are not shared with any third party.

thirdParty.title: Third-Party Cookies
items: TaskOps does not load any third-party scripts, analytics tools, advertising networks, or social media embeds.
No third-party cookies are set when you use TaskOps.

whyNoBanner.title: Why There Is No Cookie Banner
whyNoBanner.content: Since TaskOps does not use cookies — neither its own nor from third parties — there is no need for a cookie consent banner. The local storage we use is limited to essential functionality and does not require consent under current regulations.

contact.title: Contact
contact.contentWithEmail: For inquiries, you can reach us at {{contactEmail}}.
contact.contentPending: Contact information will be provided here once available.

hosting.title: Hosting
items: Database and authentication services are provided by Supabase.
The application may be hosted on various cloud platforms.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
items: This is a personal project provided as-is, without warranty of any kind.
The operator assumes no liability for the accuracy, completeness, or usefulness of any information provided through this service.
